_doc: Seed manifest for the X-Dimensional Paradigm proof-of-concept. Each claim is rendered LIVE in the browser by /x-dimensional/proof-runner.js. Nothing on the proof pages is pre-rendered or simulated.
name: x-dimensional-paradigm-proof

categories.mathematical: Tautology under the axioms — true by construction. Verifiable across all instances; not 'discovered'.
categories.architectural: Falsifiable claim about the codebase. Disprovable by static analysis or grep.
categories.behavioral: Falsifiable claim about runtime behavior. Disprovable by observation.
categories.definitional: Stipulation, not prediction. Not falsifiable. Foundational by choice.

claims.title: Public Registry Contract
claims.statement: Every entry in the public game registry shipped to the browser exposes the public-safe contract (id, name, path, entry, multiplayer, bridge) — no internal wiring fields.
claims.falsification: A shipped registry entry that omits one of the contract fields, or that exposes wiring fields beyond the public surface, disproves this claim.

evidence.asserts: internal axiom holds at compile time on every source manifest
shipped registry entries satisfy the public-safe contract

claims.title: Substrate Purity
claims.statement: For each declared substrate (Gyroid, Schwarz Diamond, Schwarz Primitive), repeated calls with the same inputs return bit-identical outputs.
claims.falsification: A single (input, output_a, output_b) triple where output_a !== output_b for the same input disproves this claim for that substrate.

claims.title: Determinism Across Reload
claims.statement: Substrate outputs at fixed coordinates are bit-identical across cold page reloads (no warm-cache bias, no random initialization).
claims.falsification: A previously-recorded output value (persisted in localStorage on first visit) differs from the freshly-computed value on a later reload, for the same substrate at the same coordinates.

evidence.asserts: surface functions are pure: const gyroid = (x,y,z) => ...

claims.title: Manifest Is the Source of Truth
claims.statement: Each game's runtime parameters trace back to a declarative manifest, not to hardcoded constants in game JS.
claims.falsification: A game whose JS hardcodes a value that is also declared in its manifest disproves this claim for that game.

evidence.asserts: every declared param is consumed by game JS; game JS reads its manifest at runtime

claims.title: Algebraic Invertibility
claims.statement: For declared algebraic relations (z=xy, z=xy^2, z=x/y, z=x/y^2, m=xyz), the inverse extractions hold within float tolerance.
claims.falsification: Any (x, y) pair where z = x*y but extract_x(z, y) does not recover x, or extract_y(z, x) does not recover y, disproves this claim.
live_inputs: Math.random()-seeded sample tuples (visible in the bloom output)

evidence.asserts: z=xy invertibility: x = z/y and y = z/x recover identities; family cross-consistency
RELATIONS table: z=xy, z=x/y, z=xy^2, z=x/y^2, x=yz, x=y/z, y=xz, y=x/z, m=xyz

claims.title: Observability of State
claims.statement: Game runtime state visible to the user is derivable by re-evaluating the substrate — not stored separately and replayed.
claims.falsification: A game whose visible state cannot be reconstructed by re-evaluating its substrate at the same observer position disproves this claim for that game.
claims.scope_admission: Currently proven in full for one game; partial for others where only the substrate layer has been audited; not yet proven for the remainder. This is a transparent admission, not theatre.

evidence.asserts: color is derived from manifold observation, not assumed

non_falsifiable_axioms.title: Manifold Axiom
non_falsifiable_axioms.statement: A manifold is an expression-shape; computation is extraction from this shape.
non_falsifiable_axioms.honest_framing: This is a stipulation about what we mean by 'manifold' in this paradigm. It cannot be empirically falsified; it can only be accepted, rejected, or refined as a definition.

non_falsifiable_axioms.title: Identity First
non_falsifiable_axioms.statement: Nothing proceeds until identity (x) is clear.
non_falsifiable_axioms.honest_framing: An ordering rule on the framework, not a prediction about the world.

non_falsifiable_axioms.title: Roles, Not Types
non_falsifiable_axioms.statement: x, y, z are roles in a frame, not fixed types of value.
non_falsifiable_axioms.honest_framing: A rule about how the labels are applied in any given frame; a value qualifies as 'x' iff some observer treats it as identity in that frame.
